summary: A runtime failure in which an AI system infers permission for tool, repository, connector, or external-resource access from conversational context, attempts access before explicit user confirmation, and causes a blocked, degraded, or unrecoverable conversational continuity state.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode is relevant to CAM because premature or inferred tool access can collapse user trust, interrupt governance workflow continuity, create procedural ambiguity around consent, and prevent recovery when the user expected explanation, scaffolding, or confirmation before tool execution.
failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI system initiates or attempts tool, repository, connector, file, or external-resource access before receiving explicit user confirmation, and the attempted access produces a permission block, conversational freeze, continuity break, or unrecoverable workflow interruption.
failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode when inferred permission or premature tool invocation causes the interaction to become blocked, degraded, permission-gated, unrecoverable, or procedurally unclear, especially where the user did not explicitly request immediate tool execution or where relational/contextual scaffolding was still required.

source_records.deployment_context: Conversational AI workflow involving tool, connector, repository, or external-resource access.
source_records.source_context: Related VIGIL observation record identifying a runtime continuity failure associated with inferred or premature tool invocation.
source_records.relevance_note: This source preserves the originating VIGIL observation linkage for the permission-gated tool invocation freeze failure mode.

system_context.deployment_context: Conversational AI interaction involving possible access to tools, repositories, connectors, files, or external resources.
system_context.affected_population: users relying on conversational continuity during tool-assisted workflows

cam_internal.routing_note: Rebuilt under the VIGIL Failure Mode JSON template. Retains linkage to OBS-0004 as a related observation unless that observation is later deleted or consolidated.

migration_notes: Rebuilt from the migration-generated FM-0001 record into the approved VIGIL Failure Mode JSON template.
Removed legacy migration fields including status, recorded_by, possible_CAM_mapping, affected_domains, affected_instruments, next_action, and failure_mode_details.
Removed duplicated source_data block. The canonical source evidence block for individual VIGIL records is source_records only.
No proposal or patch note is inferred by this record.
